test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
As used in the text, what does the word "truly" most nearly mean?
The following text is from Lilliam Rivera's 2020 novel *Never Look Back*. The text describes the narrator's friend Pheus, a young musician, after finishing a performance during a church service.
Pheus pulls a handkerchief from his back pocket and wipes his forehead. I mime a quiet clap for him. He does a slight bow and flashes his dimples. This is where Pheus <u>truly</u> shines. When Pheus is in front of an audience, it's as if he becomes another person, a more heightened version of himself.
©2020 by Lilliam Rivera
question_text: As used in the text, what does the word "truly" most nearly mean?

Often, Marzan doesn't have a specific ______ what the final product will look like when he starts a new piece. Instead, he makes up his designs as he weaves, allowing inspiration to take him in unexpected directions.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
As used in the text, what does the word "consideration" most nearly mean?
The following text is from Shirley Jackson's 1951 novel *Hangsaman*.
Natalie was leaving for her first year in college a week after her brother went back to high school; sometimes twenty-one days resolved itself into three weeks, and seemed endless; sometimes it seemed a matter of minutes slipping by so swiftly that there would never be time to approach college with appropriate <u>consideration</u>, to form a workable personality to take along.
©1951 by Shirley Jackson
question_text: As used in the text, what does the word "consideration" most nearly mean?

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice best describes the main purpose of the text?
The Marsalis family is often referred to as "the first family of jazz." Four of the children of celebrated pianist Ellis Marsalis Jr. went on to become performers who are also critically acclaimed: drummer Jason, trombonist Delfeayo, saxophonist Branford, and trumpeter Wynton. Wynton notably went on to become the first jazz composer to win the Pulitzer Prize for Music (for his oratorio *Blood on the Fields*).
question_text: Which choice best describes the main purpose of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To explain why a family of musicians received a nickname.

choices.label: B
choices.text: To compare the artistic achievements of several musicians.

choices.label: C
choices.text: To show why a particular musician won a major award.

choices.label: D
choices.text: To emphasize why musicians should work together.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?
Community science, which involves professional scientists collaborating with members of the public to study a topic, is often an effective and engaging way to conduct research. <u>It can increase the amount of data researchers can collect, offer insight into the daily life of a scientist, and spark youth interest in science.</u> This approach was essential to the success of biologist Abbigail Merrill and colleagues' study of how weather relates to a butterfly's flower choice, which included findings from hundreds of students and community members in northwestern Arkansas.
question_text: Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It offers details to clarify the claim made in the previous sentence.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It establishes the limits of the conclusion presented in the sentence that follows.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It provides a contrast with an idea presented in the previous sentence.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It identifies the purpose of the study described in the sentence that follows.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
The author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 both discuss which topic?
Text 1
Scientists can learn a lot about the Hispaniolan ground sloth and other prehistoric sloths from their fossils. But it can be hard to study sloths alive today. In the wild, the Linnaeus's two-toed sloth and other sloths spend most of their time in trees. They're difficult to observe because of their excellent camouflage and slow movements.
Text 2
Rebecca Cliffe and other scientists can now record the previously hidden activities of tree-dwelling sloths by using a backpack monitor. Such monitors can provide information to correct misconceptions. It was long believed that sloths are slow because of laziness. But, in fact, sloths' slow movements are useful. Being slow protects them from predators with keen eyesight.
question_text: The author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 both discuss which topic?

choices.label: C
choices.text: The techniques scientists use to care for sloths.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The differences between prehistoric and living sloths.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
According to the text, what are New Caledonian crows known for?
Humans aren't the only ones who use tools. Other animals also find tools helpful. Octopuses use two halves of a seashell to provide protection or a place to hide. And despite sometimes being thought of as simple, many birds make clever use of tools as well. New Caledonian crows are well known for creating hooks and spears from twigs. Furthermore, palm cockatoos have been observed using leaves to pad their beaks when opening nuts.
question_text: According to the text, what are New Caledonian crows known for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They have larger brains than palm cockatoos have.

choices.label: B
choices.text: They live longer than octopuses do.

choices.label: C
choices.text: They create tools out of twigs.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
According to the text, what is the relationship between Kenny and Lysyanskaya?
Women like Minnie McNeal Kenny made important early contributions to the history of US cryptology, a field concerned with secure data communication and storage. Kenny worked for the National Security Agency (NSA) and received the NSA's two highest awards. She also held administrative positions at the National Cryptologic School. In this way, Kenny and others like her helped make it possible for more women—such as Anna Lysyanskaya, who currently works in and teaches digital cryptography—to enter the field of cryptology.
question_text: According to the text, what is the relationship between Kenny and Lysyanskaya?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Minnie McNeal Kenny taught Anna Lysyanskaya how to securely manage important data.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Minnie McNeal Kenny and Anna Lysyanskaya worked together early in their careers.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Minnie McNeal Kenny founded an organization that Anna Lysyanskaya currently works for.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Minnie McNeal Kenny made earlier contributions to a field that Anna Lysyanskaya currently works within.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Information in the text best supports which statement about OFT?
Optimal foraging theory (OFT) holds that animals' foraging behaviors reflect cost-benefit trade-offs that vary by species and with dynamic ecological circumstances. One such circumstance is lunar intensity, which Mahmoud-Reza Hemami and colleagues found to be negatively associated with foraging by Iranian jerboas but Deborah J. Curtis and colleagues found to be positively associated with foraging by mongoose lemurs. This discrepancy is explicable in terms of OFT: the lemurs' greater reliance on vision means that higher lunar intensity benefits them more than it benefits the jerboas.
question_text: Information in the text best supports which statement about OFT?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It can account for observations of different species responding differently to similar ecological circumstances.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It tends to allow for a better understanding of the benefits of ecological circumstances than the costs of those circumstances.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It may be weakened by the finding that the costs and benefits associated with a particular ecological circumstance vary by species.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It can explain why some species act in accordance with cost-benefit trade-offs and others do not.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?
Bird species Average mass (kg) Capable of flight northern cassowary 44.0 no lesser rhea 19.6 no Dalmatian pelican 11.5 yes Andean condor 11.3 yes
Most bird species that are capable of flight weigh less than a kilogram (kg), which is not surprising considering the burden that extra body weight puts on a flying animal. But not all flying birds are so light, as we can see most clearly in the example of the ______
question_text: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?

choices.label: A
choices.text: northern cassowary, which has an average mass of 44.0 kg.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Andean condor, which has an average mass below 10 kg.

choices.label: C
choices.text: northern cassowary, which has a higher average mass than the lesser rhea.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Dalmatian pelican, which has an average mass of 11.5 kg.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which quotation from a study of California agriculture best supports the student's claim?
A student is writing a research paper on the history of irrigation in the United States, situating the development of Lake Yosemite (created in Merced County, California in 1888) in a larger historical context. The student claims that California's climate renders irrigation an essential component of agriculture in some parts of the state but not in others.
question_text: Which quotation from a study of California agriculture best supports the student's claim?

choices.label: A
choices.text: "While the low humidity of Southern California's desert areas makes irrigation a requirement if agriculture is to be a success there, the relatively high humidity in the northern parts of the state makes such practices less necessary."

choices.label: B
choices.text: "The irrigation system developed by the Hohokam people in the 7th century CE in what is now Arizona was simple but applied hydraulic engineering design features that are in use today throughout California."

choices.label: C
choices.text: "Sprinkler irrigation systems are a method of irrigating that requires machinery to spray water in all directions. These systems can presently be found in use throughout California."

choices.label: D
choices.text: "The usefulness of irrigation infrastructure in California today cannot be overstated, since it is the most common means of conveying water for agricultural purposes."

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to complete the text?
Terrestrial plants typically carry a negative electrical charge, while bees and other pollinators tend to accumulate a positive charge. A team of researchers therefore hypothesized that electrostatic attraction (mutual attraction between objects with opposite charges) might enhance pollen transmission between plants and their pollinator species. The team's experiments showed that the maximum positive charge of wintering honeybees is sufficiently high to facilitate electrostatic pollen transfer, a finding that suggests that ______
question_text: Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to complete the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: red mason bees and houseflies, having greater maximum charges than wintering honeybees have, should not demonstrate this capacity.

choices.label: B
choices.text: houseflies should demonstrate the opposite tendency.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the minimum positive charge necessary for a pollinator to induce electrostatic attraction must be greater than 300 pC.

choices.label: D
choices.text: red mason bees should also demonstrate this capacity.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Namibia has classified the house sparrow as an invasive species that could harm some of the country's native species. But researchers Alejandro Camacho and Jason McLachlan have pointed out that "invasive" and "native" are labels that describe temporary circumstances. Changes in Earth's climate may force animals from their current ranges. Climate changes may also create good habitats in areas where a species couldn't live previously. These observations suggest that ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: changes in Earth's climate are likely to result in the house sparrow establishing itself as a native species in locations other than Namibia.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Namibia was previously home to some house sparrows but they were outcompeted by invading species.

choices.label: C
choices.text: it's appropriate for Namibia to designate certain species as invasive but the country should designate the house sparrow as native.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Namibia may eventually need to reexamine its designation of the house sparrow as invasive.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Password strength meters (PSMs) provide real-time feedback to users during the password-creation process about how secure a potential password is. PSMs often include "nudges"—design elements meant to encourage the creation of robust passwords. Other PSMs provide direct guidance on how to create strong passwords. A research team found that the strongest passwords resulted from a combination of these approaches. The team also found that the specific type of nudge (reciprocity, compensation, etc.) did not significantly affect user behavior. It therefore follows that when creating passwords, users receiving ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: both compensation and reciprocity nudges would not be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving reciprocity nudges only, although users receiving direct guidance would be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving either type of nudge.

choices.label: B
choices.text: direct guidance would not be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving compensation nudges, although users receiving compensation nudges would be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving reciprocity nudges.

choices.label: C
choices.text: reciprocity nudges would not be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving compensation nudges, although users receiving both direct guidance and reciprocity nudges would be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving only reciprocity nudges.

choices.label: D
choices.text: both direct guidance and reciprocity nudges would not be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving reciprocity nudges only, although users receiving this combination would be expected to create stronger passwords than users receiving both direct guidance and compensation nudges.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
The student wants to compare the lengths of the two rivers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• The Dnieper River is in Europe.
• It ranks No. 48 among the longest rivers in the world.
• It is 2,287 kilometers long.
• The Indus River is in Asia.
• It ranks No. 19 among the longest rivers in the world.
• It is 3,610 kilometers long.
question_text: The student wants to compare the lengths of the two rivers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The Indus River is in Asia, whereas the Dnieper River is located in Europe.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The Dnieper River is shorter than the Indus River.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The Dnieper River in Europe is 2,287 kilometers long.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Among the longest rivers in the world, the Indus River is ranked No. 19.

test_name: October 2025 International Form A SAT English Module 1
passage:
The student wants to make and support a claim about Iberian orca behavior. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• 2024: Spain and Portugal sponsored a workshop to address recent encounters between Iberian orcas and marine vessels off the Iberian Peninsula.
• Many of the 637 documented incidents involved pods of orcas damaging vessels by ramming, nudging, or biting the rudders.
• Studies of Iberian orcas suggest recent increases in tuna populations have reduced the time the orcas spend hunting by 99%.
• Researchers believe this shift has increased the number of interactions between marine vessels and understimulated orcas.
• The workshop advised mariners to avoid orcas pending further testing of the efficacy of TAST, a harmless acoustic deterrent.
question_text: The student wants to make and support a claim about Iberian orca behavior. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The recent destructive behavior of Iberian orcas may be the result of understimulation, given that orcas' interactions with marine vessels have increased as the orcas have spent less time hunting.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Tuna populations have increased by approximately 99% off the Iberian Peninsula due to changes in the hunting behavior of Iberian orcas.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As supported by the workshop's analysis of 637 encounters between vessels and Iberian orcas, TAST is a harmless and effective acoustic deterrent.

choices.label: D
choices.text: In 2024, Spain and Portugal sponsored a workshop that addressed incidents where pods of Iberian orcas were ramming, nudging, or biting the rudders of vessels.
